en.extract:
The vinous-breasted sparrowhawk (Tachyspiza rhodogaster) is a species of bird of prey in the family Accipitridae. It was formerly placed in the genus Accipiter. It is endemic to the island of Sulawesi in Indonesia. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest, subtropical or tropical mangrove forest, and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.


Diet
It eats small passerine birds.

The '''vinous-breasted sparrowhawk''' ('''''Tachyspiza rhodogaster''''') is a species of [[bird of prey]] in the family [[Accipitridae]]. It was formerly placed in the [[genus]] ''[[Accipiter]]''. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the island of [[Sulawesi]] in [[Indonesia]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]], subtropical or tropical [[mangrove forest]], and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]].

== Diet ==
It eats small passerine birds.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=O'Connell |first1=Darren P. |last2=Kelly |first2=David J. |last3=Kelly |first3=Seán B. A. |last4=Sealy |first4=Síofra |last5=Karya |first5=Adi |last6=Analuddin |first6=Kangkuso |last7=Marples |first7=Nicola M. |date=2019-03-21 |title=Increased sexual dimorphism in dense populations of Olive-backed Sunbirds on small islands: morphological niche contraction in females but not males |url=https://figshare.com/ndownloader/files/14668517 |journal=Emu - Austral Ornithology |volume=119 |issue=3 |pages=296–307 |doi=10.1080/01584197.2019.1588743 |s2cid=109925310 |issn=0158-4197|url-access=subscription }}</ref>
